summary: Verified Auto-Capture Sequence feature end-to-end on Meters view: modal opens, scope/level/dwell/settle controls behave correctly, sweep runs through all 16 PHY outputs, cancel mid-run leaves partial rows, CSV download fires with correct filename pattern, readonly disables Start, and all regression buttons coexist.
critical_code_review_comments: AutoCaptureSequence.start() mutates dspStore for every output then immediately reads `state.outputs` snapshot — fine because closure captures pre-mutation snapshot, but if the store ever becomes async this could race. Consider using functional updates or capturing snapshot via store.getState() to be future-proof.

Sequence does NOT await the React state-update tick after updateOutputDeep before sampling meters. In live audio this is OK (audio graph updates synchronously via audioEngine), but in the headless test environment all peak values are -∞ (AudioContext suspended). Not a bug — expected — but worth a comment in the file.
Settle slider step=50 ms but min=100; pressing Home in the test correctly produced 100 ms. No issue.
downloadCsv uses URL.createObjectURL + a.click() without appending to document — works in modern Chromium but some browsers (Firefox) require the anchor to be in the DOM. Low priority.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Auto-Capture Sequence (iteration 14) is fully functional. Test invariants verified: row count == scope size (16 for phy, 32 for all), state restoration after cancel (mute buttons not stuck), CSV filename pattern 'auto-capture-phy-*.csv', readonly disables ac-start (disabled attribute present + opacity-30 class). Peak dB values are −∞ in headless because AudioContext is suspended without user gesture — that is expected and not a bug. All regression items (open-snapshot, meters-popout, pn-type-pink/white/sweep) still present. Test used ac-dwell=500 / ac-settle=100 to keep runtime ~10 s.
